metadata.name: agg_hours_per_project
metadata.comment: Total hours per project from the clean fact mart. project_id is a DB-enforced primary key (the aggregation grain); total_hours is NOT NULL by construction.

project_id.name: project_id
project_id.comment: Project PK (DB-enforced). One row per project that has timesheets (21 rows).

timesheet_count.name: timesheet_count
timesheet_count.comment: Number of accepted timesheet entries for this project.

total_hours.name: total_hours
total_hours.comment: Total logged hours for this project (sum of fct_timesheets.hours). NOT NULL by construction.

distinct_employees.name: distinct_employees
distinct_employees.comment: Count of distinct employees who logged hours on this project.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: agg_hours_per_project_monthly
metadata.comment: Monthly hours per project (project x month). Adds the time dimension to the per-project rollup, revealing each project's burn-down/tempo over time. One row per (project_id, month). Reads fct_timesheets.

project_id.name: project_id
project_id.comment: Project identifier; part of the composite PK (project_id, month).

month.name: month
month.comment: First day of the month (date_trunc('month', date)); part of the composite PK. One row per project-month with timesheets.

timesheet_count.name: timesheet_count
timesheet_count.comment: Number of accepted timesheet entries for this project in this month.

total_hours.name: total_hours
total_hours.comment: Total logged hours for this project in this month.

distinct_employees.name: distinct_employees
distinct_employees.comment: Count of distinct employees who logged hours on this project in this month.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: dim_employees
metadata.comment: Clean employee dimension: one row per employee_id from stg_employees (the dedup survivors). Soft-issue rows (null name E013, null role E021) are retained since the id is valid. Contracted; employee_id is a DB-enforced primary key and the FK target for fct_timesheets.

employee_id.name: employee_id
employee_id.comment: Employee PK (DB-enforced primary key). One row per employee_id (40 rows).

name.name: name
name.comment: Employee name; nullable (NULL for E013 — review row kept).

role.name: role
role.comment: Employee role; nullable (NULL for E021 — review row kept). One of 8 valid roles (accepted_values source test).

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: dim_projects
metadata.comment: Clean project dimension: one row per project_id from stg_projects (the dedup survivors). Soft-issue rows (null name P013, null/invalid budget P008/P032/P019) are retained since the id is valid. Negative budget is nullified here. Contracted; project_id is a DB-enforced primary key and the FK target for fct_timesheets.

project_id.name: project_id
project_id.comment: Project PK (DB-enforced primary key). One row per project_id (35 rows).

project_name.name: project_name
project_name.comment: Project name; nullable (NULL for P013 — review row kept).

budget.name: budget
budget.comment: Project budget as decimal(12,2); NULL for P008 (non-numeric source), P019 (negative, nullified), P032 (null source). Review rows kept with NULL budget.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: fct_timesheets
metadata.comment: Clean timesheets fact mart: the accepted fact set from int_timesheets_validated left-joined to dim_employees and dim_projects. Pure join, no filters — int already removed dangling FKs and single-source rejects, so the grain is clean (one row per timesheet_id). timesheet_id is a DB-enforced primary key; employee_id / project_id FK integrity is enforced upstream by int_timesheets_validated (prevention) and asserted here by relationships tests (detection).

timesheet_id.name: timesheet_id
timesheet_id.comment: Fact PK (DB-enforced primary key). Deterministic md5 surrogate of the natural key (employee_id|project_id|date|hours). One row per accepted timesheet (331 rows).

employee_id.name: employee_id
employee_id.comment: Employee FK → dim_employees.employee_id. Guaranteed valid (dangling rows filtered in int_timesheets_validated). Asserted by relationships test.

employee_name.name: employee_name
employee_name.comment: Denormalized employee name from dim_employees (for query convenience).

employee_role.name: employee_role
employee_role.comment: Denormalized employee role from dim_employees (for query convenience).

project_id.name: project_id
project_id.comment: Project FK → dim_projects.project_id. Guaranteed valid (dangling rows filtered in int_timesheets_validated). Asserted by relationships test.

project_name.name: project_name
project_name.comment: Denormalized project name from dim_projects (for query convenience).

project_budget.name: project_budget
project_budget.comment: Denormalized project budget from dim_projects (for query convenience). NULL for review projects.

date.name: date
date.comment: Timesheet date; non-null (bad dates removed in staging). Parsed from 3 source formats (ISO / dd/mm/yyyy / dd-Mon-yy).

hours.name: hours
hours.comment: Hours worked; non-null, in range (0, 24] (non-numeric and out-of-range removed in staging).

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: int_timesheets_validated
metadata.comment: The accepted fact set: stg_timesheets semi-joined to the clean stg_employees / stg_projects (IN subqueries, no fan-out) to filter dangling FKs. Feeds fct_timesheets. The only intermediate (connects multiple atomic sources).

timesheet_id.name: timesheet_id
timesheet_id.comment: Deterministic surrogate PK (md5 of natural key) from stg_timesheets; one per accepted timesheet row.

employee_id.name: employee_id
employee_id.comment: Employee FK; guaranteed non-dangling after the semi-join filter to stg_employees.

project_id.name: project_id
project_id.comment: Project FK; guaranteed non-dangling after the semi-join filter to stg_projects.

date.name: date
date.comment: Parsed timesheet date (ISO / dd/mm/yyyy / dd-Mon-yy); non-null and valid (bad dates removed in staging).

hours.name: hours
hours.comment: Hours worked; non-null, in range (0, 24] (non-numeric and out-of-range removed in staging).

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: rpt_project_economics
metadata.comment: Per-project economics: budget + logged hours + derived budget_per_hour (budget / total_hours). LEFT-joins from dim_projects so ALL projects appear (budgeted-but-not-started show 0 hours). ASSUMPTION: budget_per_hour is meaningful only if budget is a labor-cost budget; otherwise indicative only.

project_id.name: project_id
project_id.comment: Project PK (DB-enforced). One row per project (35 rows, including projects with no timesheets).

budget.name: budget
budget.comment: Project budget from dim_projects. NULL for review projects (P008/P019/P032).

total_hours.name: total_hours
total_hours.comment: Total logged hours for this project (0 for budgeted-but-not-started projects).

distinct_employees.name: distinct_employees
distinct_employees.comment: Count of distinct employees who logged hours (0 for projects with no timesheets).

budget_per_hour.name: budget_per_hour
budget_per_hour.comment: Derived: budget / total_hours. NULL when total_hours = 0 or budget is NULL. Assumes budget is a labor-cost budget (see model description).

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: stg_employees
metadata.comment: Typed, deduped projection of raw.employees: one row per non-null employee_id (dup_rank = 1 survivor). Soft-issue rows (null name/role) are KEPT since the id is valid.

employee_id.name: employee_id
employee_id.comment: Clean employee identifier (non-null; exact duplicates removed).

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: stg_projects
metadata.comment: Typed, deduped projection of raw.projects: one row per non-null project_id (dup_rank = 1 survivor). try_cast budget to decimal (non-numeric -> NULL). Soft-issue rows (null name, null/invalid/negative budget) are KEPT; negative budget is nullified in dim_projects.

project_id.name: project_id
project_id.comment: Clean project identifier (non-null; exact duplicates removed).

project_name.name: project_name
project_name.comment: Project name (NULL where the source field was empty -- P013).

budget.name: budget
budget.comment: Budget as decimal(12,2); non-numeric source values (e.g. '55000-60000' for P008) -> NULL. Negative (P019) preserved here, nullified in dim_projects.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: stg_timesheets
metadata.comment: Typed, deduped projection of raw.timesheets with single-source hard rejects removed (missing employee_id, unparseable date, non-numeric/out-of-range hours). Cross-source dangling FKs (E099/E050/P999) are KEPT here and removed in int_timesheets_validated. Adds a deterministic surrogate PK timesheet_id.

employee_id.name: employee_id
employee_id.comment: Employee identifier (non-null; single-source rejects with null employee_id removed; dangling E099/E050 retained).

date.name: date
date.comment: Parsed date via coalesce(try_cast date, try_strptime %d/%m/%Y, try_strptime %d-%b-%y); unparseable rows removed.

hours.name: hours
hours.comment: Hours as decimal(5,2); non-numeric rows removed, out-of-range (<=0 or >24) rows removed.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table
